item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah uh what the fuck werillyium

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally do not like this as it feels very rushed and unfinished. While I do like the overall concept, I can't help but feel that this should belong on the Log of Anomalous Items rather than a mainlist SCP.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly one of those great, simple articles that I wish there were more of. Easy to read, casual, and the story doesn't feel like I'm reading a novel. Also, after hearing all the references to "salsa hammer" in the IRC, it was nice to realize that it was such a great article, not just some infamous thing everyone hated.

+1, mega cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Y'know, I think this is one of my favourite SCPs. There's very few SCPs that I like to come back to and read again multiple times and this is one of them.

It very successfully creates something quite horrific using a 50 word addendum and that is very impressive.

I'm actually gonna say that I don't think this would work on the Log of Anomalous Items. It feels like it really needs the space of a full SCP and the addendum to work and converting them into the Log format wouldn't have the time for the same satisfying ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: honestly, salsa's more of a scoop than a dip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
""Any situation that would reduce a character's head to the consistency of chunky salsa dip is fatal, regardless of other rules."

And you managed to deliberately break this rule with a pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: because its good and great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, I posted it there according to the instructions in the guides

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how did this get on the main list???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait a sec… Say a person is hit in the abdomen and there is fecal matter in the AoE. Would the feces be converted into salsa too?!?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If anyone's got a fresh and spicy idea for the termination log, by all means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know it might seem like a bit of a cliche, but couldn't this be used as a means to exterminate SCP-682 (The Hard-To-Destroy Reptile)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please write this! Your idea is amazing and I NEED this to happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there some punchline I'm missing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow! Thank you, and glad you liked it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found this scip randomly and found it so funny I translated it to French right away.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lets beat 682 to death with it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're the first person to have a thought-out, concrete reason to repeat the line. I'm still not gonna do it, because personal preference, but thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually quite like this (for the implication of survival post-brain consumption, and simulation of internal organs via salsa) — but as others have pointed out, this feels like it would fit far better on the log of anomalous items. Not just because it's short — but because that format would let you get to the setup and punchline so much faster.

This is almost pitch perfect re: what the log is for. You could do it in two or three lines there, and it would have just as much impact. I get not wanting to put it there for lack of exposure, though (plus you lose the great picture).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First person to correctly pinpoint the inspiration, gold star for you. I'll see what I can do to clarify that a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A person after my own heart. Glad you enjoyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thematic coherence is an overrated trick. Sometimes the sheer dissonance is the charm. I dare say the article would be way more boring if the item were a spoon or bowl or cutting board.

It's a sledgehammer purely because I find the mental image of bludgeoning a guy over the head with one, and salsa instead of viscera splattering everywhere, hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you're looking for a different word than "meta" but alright. It's different because I decided I wanted to post it on the main list. There's no magical cutoff or criteria for whether something "belongs" on the anomalous item log or here.

And this isn't directed solely at you, but I'm really starting to get fed up with the general stifling of creativity going on with regard to "simple" SCPs that are just "things that do a thing".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A sauce-erer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You misunderstand, I was not calling it meta cause it's short, im calling the article in general meta. I got no issues with short skips. I called it meta cause it's a thing that does a thing. No discovery log, no test logs (admittedly I can see why), but in general it's really just a thing that does a thing.

Im not calling it bad, but how is this any different than items in the anomalous items log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for the chunky salsa rule. Although since the subjects are still alive, I guess this is more of a subversion.

I had to read through the comments before I realized that the salsa is still somehow performing all of the functions of the tissue it replaces, including thinking and living, until it gets destroyed. I feel like that could have been better explained in the article itself. Still, clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this.
So many new post are these super long, 20 page stories, where the anomaly isn't all that wierd, like portals, monsters, and memetics. But I still love simple weird anomalies.
And what is weirder than a salsa hammer?
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: did you just

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clearly further tasting — er, testing, is required.

The Foundation in EoD could use some of their in-house wizard squad to try and establish telepathic communication with an ultra-aged person who'd been transmogrified into salsa. Then they would learn direct from the person whether salsafication were a net improvement. Or maybe there's an in-universe sciencey way of doing it, like a tricorder that measures their Elan Vital Energy or somesuch.

Then just to be extra cautious, they could have junior researchers and/or the junior wizard squad go out and randomly test some off-the-shelf salsa for traces of consciousness. And what if they found some?

What if 3794 isn't unique? What if there's an evil salsa sorcerer who's still out there? O_o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The weird thing is that I don't even like salsa all that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this being better suited for the anomalous item log, and not simply because it is short.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weryllium what on earth is going on in your head.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just the right amount of stupid with that ending punchline almost slipping right past me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you or have you ever been salsa? Do you know if salsa feels pain?

Who is to decide what that tasty tomato stuff does and does not feel but the subject itself? It may not even feel pain in a way we comprehend, but rather a pain specific to salsa and salsa-adjacent products.

I think therefore I am, and we currently have no proof that salsa does not think

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems poor at first, but the effets are in fact a bit more complex. But it the same at the end. It is just strange for me.

I don't get the link between and hammer and salsa. It looks like you take a random object to write this article. An other thing, more relate with salsa, should be more coherent.

I also don't get why people eat the brain of the subject. For scientific purpose ? There was a lot of things that we can do on this anomaly, but we only choose to eat the brain of a man. Maybe a side effect ? We can't know, as readers.

At the end, it seems not enough developped for me. I don't like it. Down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After 30 minutes he'd still be in the stomach but yeah, kudos for getting the gist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno how on earth this could possibly be meta. I've been doing short stuff long before it was a trend and I'll do it long after it stops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, short skips and all.
To me it just feels too meta.
And something that may just belong better in say, the anomalous items log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reeks of 2398's simple yet effective premise but with the absurdity of having salsa in it.

I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My initial reaction was “Okay, somebody ate the brain salsa and then died… but why? The salsa the hammer creates is supposedly normal, not poisonous or anything…”

Then I realized it was the subject who died. A full 30 minutes later. He survived for a half hour while his “brain” was being digested.

Upvoted as hard as possible once the realization hit me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow um ok then +1

epic reference
like and share for epic Mother 3 reference

Why wouldn't it turn them into a sad monkey???? hmmmmmmmm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But in End of Death it'd make the ultra-aged much easier to store. And I'd be willing to bet salsa does not feel pain, so it might be a net improvement to be hammered into salsa and squeegeed into a nice clean jar instead of stuck in a decaying-yet-alive human body.

If one were attacked by some sort of implacable enemies, turning their arms into salsa so they couldn't use weapons and/or magic against you would be hilarious.

Alternatively, I can think of at least two cannibal restaurants which would probably be delighted to serve People Salsa.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Salsa man! Probably a bad idea to eat him tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like not enough is being said about the stinger here:

Consumption of the salsa resulted in the death of the subject within 30 minutes.

So his brain was salsa, then his brain was poop, and then he died. This whole skip makes me feel like my brain is made of salsa. +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i can't believe the shit y'all just decide to write

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ye, I realized post comment, still generally wasn't the article for me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how short it is and I always enjoy short skips. Those are a rarity, but I can't help but feel conflicted with this article. It doesn't feel complete. Like, it is missing a few sentences. It feels more like notes outlining an SCP.

Nonetheless, I give my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It definitely wouldn’t kill anyone, especially if their consciousness now resides within the Salsa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts the same.

Eh, screw it. While I still think it's a bit too short, its well executed enough and the article's grown on me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now, I have to say I like this. It's short, simple, weird and does everything it has to.

But at the same time, I'm afraid I can't agree with it being an SCP article - it doesn't do enough for me to agree with it being anything more than just an anomalous item.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three thoughts fight in my head for precedence upon reading this skip:

1. Who the HELL in-universe would create such a thing and how big a trophy shall we get them

2. Someone give this thing to Lolly the Clown I wanna see what happens

3. Holy crap what would the Foundation do with this in End of Death

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know. I novoted. And then I realised they clobbered a dude in the head, and then intentionally ate his head salsa. And they play that off as completely normal. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know…
Someone strike some things with it whilst I'm around
Bad things……….. like suicide by salsa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: turn him to chunky salsa, boys

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really trying to think of a good mjolnir pun that somehow incorporates salsa, but I can't hammer anything out because I'm a dip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think weryllium probably knows what he's doing. He's no greenhorn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fantastic bite-sized black humour. I normally wouldn't be a fan of such a laconic article, but this hits the spot in a strange way.

+1 the madman did it again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just an object that does something and doesn't have anything else to back it up, you should have run this through the drafts and critiques forum, especially since as you explained, it came to you last night late and you more or less scrapped together this in a small amount of time.
-1

item.title: First skip of the new year
item.content:encoded:
Came to me at 3 am last night. 9Volt and Kakroom said it didn't suck.

Pic is from https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sledgehammer_(33104910115).jpg CC 2.0 generic.

On the off chance that whoever left a draft in here is still around and still wants it, I've copied it below.

blah
– hide block

Item# : SCP 6969

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: Item must be kept in a 3x3x3 meter room, the room must be lined with high quality sound muffling material. The room must be installed with an air-lock system. The room must be vacumed when not in use.

In fear of witnessing "The Smile" of the object, Video recording or surveillance of the item’s screen is strictly prohibited, any unauthorized personnel attempts to do so, will be detained and remove from the site forcefully.

Recording of the sound must be done by two authorized personnel in a sound proof room, one of the personnel shall write any data that is deemed useful on an A4 sized lined paper. Conversations with the item or sayings from the test subject(s) shall be written in script format. The scientific personnel need to wear ear mufflers and watch over the D-Class personnel. If the D-Class are acting irregularly then the personnel must turn off the recording and take the D-Class for a full mental survey.

Listening to the item's song for more than 10 minutes is not recommended. In addition, test subjects that has been with the item for more than 25 minutes with the item must be killed as they become SCP-6969-02

In case of containment breach of SCP- 6969-02 with SCP-6969, combat personel must first wear ear mufflers before engaging and eradicating SCP 6969-02. After eradicating SCP-6969-02, cover SCP- 6969 with an opaque fabric then carry it on to SCP-6969, bring it back to it's cell and place it back on the table, then call in a D class personnel to set it up right on the phone stand provided before removing the cloak.

Description: SCP-6969 is an Apple branded touch screen phone playing looped video of a person of an unspecified gender due to the person portraying as both female and male dancing to a song starting off with the words "Hit or Miss" (see Addendum SCP 6969-B). However, it has been confirmed by one of the test subject (see Addendum
SCP 6969-B) that the person is wearing a female cheerleader attire.

When listening to SCP-6969 song, reports have shown that the victim experiences the following symptoms: Absent-Mindedness, fatigued or irregular muscle spasms.When watching SCP-6969 looped video, victims describe that they feel relaxed and awestruck. After an extended period of time spent with SCP-6969, victim will not respond to any vocal or audible stimuli, when attempt to make physical contact victim will retaliate,
at this point the victim can now be confirmed as SCP-6969-02

SCP-6969 was found on ██/█/2018 at █████ street in California, ██████ when Agent █████, which was off duty at the time, and is now dead, found a group of people staring at an iPhone (SCP- 6969).
Agent █████ was killed due to him becoming SCP 6969-02, Agent █████ brought SCP 6969 to the site, laughing and having spasms, he was under the influence of SCP 6969.

Addendum SCP 6969-B:

Transcript between Dr.█████ and D-1783:

Dr.█████: D-1783, explain what exactly you see?
D-1783: It's, an iPhone? An iPhone X.
Dr.█████: Alright
D-1783: I feel…so [Incoherent mumbling]
Dr.█████: Can you please repeat?
[ No response from D-1783]
Dr.█████: Are you there, D-1783?
[ No respons from D-1783]
Dr.█████: Answer me D-1783 or we will have to send a security guard to terminate you.
D-1783: That……
Dr.█████: What are you seeing?
D-1783: That…. smile…. its perfect
Dr.█████: Can you describe what you are seeing, D-1783?
Unclear audio, song can be made out to be iLOVEFRiDAY - Mia Khalifa.
Dr.█████: Hello?
D-1783: I guess they never miss.. [followed by mumbling)
[End Transmission]

Officer █████ and Officer █████ (both KIA) were sent in to retrieve D-1783, When Officer █████ tried to grab D-1783, D-1783 retaliate by throwing himself onto Officer █████ and punching him in the head multiple times, causing bleeding and breaking of the skull. Officer █████ died from the breakage of his skull on the spot.. D-1783 rushed towards Officer █████, D-1783 was shot in the head.
